description: Enterprise Linux server health overview. Provides at-a-glance status for CPU, memory, disk, network, load, and systemd services across the Linux fleet. Designed as a SCOM replacement.

panels.description: Average CPU utilization across selected Linux servers. Sourced from the recording rule instance:linux_cpu_utilization:ratio.
panels.title: CPU Utilization

panels.description: Average memory utilization across selected Linux servers. Sourced from the recording rule instance:linux_memory_utilization:ratio.
panels.title: Memory Utilization

panels.description: Average uptime in days across selected Linux servers. Sourced from the recording rule instance:linux_uptime:seconds.
panels.title: Uptime

panels.description: Average normalized load (1-minute load / CPU count). A value of 1.0 means the system is fully loaded. Sourced from the recording rule instance:linux_load_normalized:ratio.
panels.title: Load Average

panels.description: Total count of failed systemd units across selected Linux servers. Sourced from the recording rule instance:linux_systemd_failed:count.
panels.title: Failed Systemd Units

panels.description: CPU utilization over time for each selected Linux server, expressed as a percentage. Uses the pre-computed recording rule for efficient querying.
panels.title: CPU Utilization Over Time

panels.description: Breakdown of CPU time by mode (user, system, iowait, idle, etc.) as a stacked area chart. Uses the raw node_cpu_seconds_total counter with rate() to show per-second utilization by mode.
panels.title: CPU Modes Breakdown

panels.description: System load averages (1m, 5m, 15m) for selected servers. Load is NOT normalized here; compare against CPU count to assess saturation.
panels.title: Load Average (1m, 5m, 15m)

panels.description: Memory utilization over time per selected server. Uses the recording rule which computes 1 - (MemAvailable / MemTotal).
panels.title: Memory Utilization Over Time

panels.description: Breakdown of physical memory into used, buffers, cached, and free components as a stacked area chart. Useful for understanding kernel memory allocation patterns.

targets.expr: avg by (hostname) (node_memory_MemTotal_bytes{hostname=~"$hostname", environment=~"$environment", datacenter=~"$datacenter"} - node_memory_MemFree_bytes{hostname=~"$hostname", environment=~"$environment", datacenter=~"$datacenter"} - node_memory_Buffers_bytes{hostname=~"$hostname", environment=~"$environment", datacenter=~"$datacenter"} - node_memory_Cached_bytes{hostname=~"$hostname", environment=~"$environment", datacenter=~"$datacenter"})

panels.title: Memory Breakdown

panels.description: Swap memory usage over time. High swap usage indicates memory pressure and potential performance degradation from disk paging.

targets.expr: node_memory_SwapTotal_bytes{hostname=~"$hostname", environment=~"$environment", datacenter=~"$datacenter", role=~"$role"} - node_memory_SwapFree_bytes{hostname=~"$hostname", environment=~"$environment", datacenter=~"$datacenter", role=~"$role"}

panels.title: Swap Usage

panels.description: Percentage of free disk space per mountpoint and hostname. Uses the recording rule which excludes tmpfs, devtmpfs, squashfs, and overlay filesystems.
panels.title: Disk Free %

panels.description: Disk I/O utilization as a percentage of time the device is busy servicing requests. Uses the recording rule based on node_disk_io_time_seconds_total. Sustained values near 100% indicate an I/O bottleneck.
panels.title: Disk I/O Utilization

panels.description: Disk read and write throughput per block device, computed from the raw counters node_disk_read_bytes_total and node_disk_written_bytes_total.
panels.title: Disk Read/Write Throughput

panels.description: Combined network throughput (receive + transmit) per server. Uses the recording rule which excludes loopback and virtual interfaces.
panels.title: Network Throughput

panels.description: Per-interface network throughput split into receive (positive) and transmit (negative Y-axis) for detailed bandwidth analysis. Excludes loopback and virtual interfaces.
panels.title: Network In/Out per Interface

panels.description: Lists all systemd units currently in a failed state. Equivalent to 'systemctl --failed' on each server. Investigate with 'systemctl status <unit>' and 'journalctl -u <unit>'.
panels.title: Failed Systemd Units

list.description: Filter by deployment environment (production, staging, etc.)
list.label: Environment
list.name: environment

list.description: Filter by datacenter or site location
list.label: Datacenter
list.name: datacenter

list.description: Filter by server hostname. Only shows Linux servers.
list.label: Hostname
list.name: hostname

list.description: Filter by server role (web, database, application, etc.)
list.label: Role
list.name: role

title: Linux Server Overview
